subject: chemistry
title: 19M.1.HL.TZ2.32
question_html:
Which compound has the lowest boiling point?

A. CH3CH2CH2CH2CH2CH3

B. CH3CH2CH2CH2CH3

C. CH3CH(CH3)CH2CH3

D. CH3C(CH3)2CH3
examiners_html: Surprisingly, this was one of the challenging questions on the paper. Only 63 % of the candidates chose dimethylpropane as the compound having the lowest boiling point. The most commonly chosen distractor was pentane (B) which did not take into account the effect of branching on the strength of London dispersion forces.
